title: Leelas Homeworld

jokes.text: Sure, we talk about it all the time. Really? No. Burn!
jokes.setup: Professor asks if they know about Leela's orphanarium
jokes.punchline: Bender sarcastically confirms then immediately admits they don't, followed by self-congratulatory 'Burn!'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - his fake engagement followed by immediate honesty and pride in his own burn is very character-specific

jokes.text: Your parents must be so proud! Sorry.
jokes.setup: Leela wins 'Orphan of the Year' award
jokes.punchline: Fry congratulates her about parents being proud, then realizes she's an orphan
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry obliviousness - perfectly in character but somewhat predictable

jokes.text: It's a glow-In-The-Dark nose you can wear over your regular non-Glowing nose.
jokes.setup: Professor reveals his 'latest invention'
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously specific and pointless invention with redundant description
jokes.explanation: Typical Professor invention absurdity - the redundant 'non-glowing nose' specification is nicely absurd

jokes.text: Now I can punch you in the nose in the dark.
jokes.setup: Professor demonstrates glowing nose invention
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately sees violent application
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - takes innocent invention and immediately thinks of violence. High character integration.

jokes.text: Where'd it go?
jokes.setup: Bender tries to punch Professor's nose in the dark
jokes.punchline: The lights go out and he can't find the glowing nose
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy payoff to the setup. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: If you think it's anything, you're a suspicious moron.
jokes.setup: Bender asks about suspicious liquid from machine
jokes.punchline: Professor's defensive overreaction reveals guilt while insulting questioner
jokes.explanation: Good Professor paranoia - the insulting deflection is character-appropriate

jokes.text: Delicious fig pudding! That's good. But a distinct aftertaste of toxic waste.
jokes.setup: Fry tastes the suspicious liquid
jokes.punchline: Initial positive reaction followed by admission it tastes like toxic waste
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry stupidity - tasting obvious toxic waste but trying to be polite about it

jokes.text: Professor, I'll take care of that waste for $499 and 100 cents.
jokes.setup: Hermes demands $500 for waste disposal
jokes.punchline: Bender offers $499 and 100 cents (which equals $500)
jokes.explanation: Clever mathematical wordplay that's very Bender - trying to sound like a better deal while being identical

jokes.text: I know That's a rip, but I'll pay for the convenience.
jokes.setup: Bender offers the exact same price with different wording
jokes.punchline: Professor acknowledges it's a ripoff but agrees for 'convenience'
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to Bender's scam - Professor's laziness overriding logic

jokes.text: Do you take credit cards? Let's find out.
jokes.setup: Professor asks about payment method
jokes.punchline: Bender's casual uncertainty about his own business practices
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender incompetence - not even knowing his own payment policies

jokes.text: All right, environment, you've met your match.
jokes.setup: Bender prepares to dispose of toxic waste
jokes.punchline: Treats environmental destruction like a personal vendetta
jokes.explanation: Excellently characterizes Bender's antagonistic relationship with everything, including nature itself

jokes.text: Honey, that thing was ruined the minute it went on you.
jokes.setup: Mutant complains about ruined wedding dress
jokes.punchline: Cruel surface dweller insults the mutant's appearance
jokes.explanation: Mean-spirited humor that establishes surface prejudice against mutants. Functional but not clever.

jokes.text: Not if I have anything to do with it.
jokes.setup: Leela says 'It's not easy being an orphan'
jokes.punchline: Authority figure at orphanarium threatens to make it harder
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about institutional negligence. Brief but effective character moment.

jokes.text: Because I have a photographic memory, and I remember every night.
jokes.setup: Says he'll never forget the night Leela was abandoned
jokes.punchline: Explains it's because he remembers literally every night
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of the emotional moment - turns touching sentiment into weird literal fact

jokes.text: Picture's up. It's crooked!
jokes.setup: Emotional moment as Leela's picture is hung with distinguished alumni
jokes.punchline: Kid interrupts touching moment with pedantic observation
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of sentimental moment. Simple but effective timing.

jokes.text: Look at me! I'm Leela! No! Because I'm double Leela! Whee!
jokes.setup: Children are energetically cheering for Leela
jokes.punchline: One kid puts something over his eye to mock Leela's cyclops appearance
jokes.explanation: Kids being insensitive about Leela's appearance. Mean-spirited and predictable.

jokes.text: That's it, Albert. No more espresso for you!
jokes.setup: Albert is acting hyperactively
jokes.punchline: Adult realizes giving espresso to orphan children was a bad idea
jokes.explanation: Absurd institutional neglect - giving caffeine to hyperactive children is wonderfully stupid

jokes.text: If those aren't tears of happiness, please stop crying.
jokes.setup: Leela is clearly sad despite winning an award
jokes.punchline: Fry's awkward attempt to handle her emotions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry social awkwardness - wants to help but doesn't know how to handle sadness

jokes.text: I usually try to keep my sadness pent up inside... Where it can fester quietly as a mental illness.
jokes.setup: Leela apologizes for showing emotion
jokes.punchline: Describes extremely unhealthy coping mechanism in clinical terms
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor about mental health. The clinical description of self-destruction is perfectly written.

jokes.text: Yeah, I do that with my stupidness.
jokes.setup: Leela describes repressing emotions as mental illness
jokes.punchline: Fry relates by admitting he represses his stupidity, using wrong grammar
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment - tries to be supportive but demonstrates his stupidity while discussing repressing it. The grammar error sells it.

jokes.text: Because I happen to be a holding, stroking, loving machine. Also spanking.
jokes.setup: Leela wants parental affection - holding, stroking, loving
jokes.punchline: Fry offers to provide it but adds 'spanking' making it creepy
jokes.explanation: Fry's well-meaning offer becomes inappropriate. Good character work but relies on sexual innuendo.

jokes.text: Yo, yo, Bazender! What up?
jokes.setup: Bender calls himself on his own phone
jokes.punchline: Uses street slang and combines 'Baz' with 'Bender'
jokes.explanation: Bender's ego and self-importance manifest as calling himself. The nickname is mildly clever.

jokes.text: I just got hired to clean up the set of Free Willy 3!
jokes.setup: Bender brags about his dumping business success
jokes.punchline: References cleaning up after a famously bad movie sequel
jokes.explanation: Simple pop culture reference joke. Relies on audience knowledge of bad sequels rather than wit.

jokes.text: Namely, I'm up here, and they are safely down--
jokes.setup: Bender dismisses mutant threat because he's above them
jokes.punchline: Gets grabbed mid-sentence, proving himself wrong
jokes.explanation: Classic 'famous last words' structure. Well-timed interruption that proves character wrong immediately.

jokes.text: Oh, man. As if our lives weren't miserable enough already.
jokes.setup: Toxic waste has made their lake too bright to hide their ugliness
jokes.punchline: Mutant's resigned acceptance that this is just another problem
jokes.explanation: Good understatement of their plight. The weary acceptance adds dark humor to their situation.

jokes.text: It's like that time I peeked in the kitchen at Imperial Hunan.
jokes.setup: Seeing the gross mutant demonstration of mutation effects
jokes.punchline: Compares horrifying mutation to bad restaurant hygiene
jokes.explanation: Great Fry comparison - making horrible situations relatable through mundane references. Good character voice.

jokes.text: Wait. I don't even have DNA. Why am I screaming?
jokes.setup: About to be submerged in DNA-mutating water
jokes.punchline: Realizes as a robot he has no DNA but has been screaming anyway
jokes.explanation: Excellent logic gag - Bender's mid-panic realization that the threat doesn't apply to him. Perfect robot/biology joke.

jokes.text: We're gonna beat you afterwards.
jokes.setup: Bender realizes mutation won't affect him
jokes.punchline: Mutant calmly explains they'll just use regular violence instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan follow-up to Bender's realization. The matter-of-fact delivery of violence threat is very funny.

jokes.text: Great Cheech's ghost!
jokes.setup: Fry needs an exclamation upon discovering the Leela shrine
jokes.punchline: References Cheech of Cheech & Chong in place of religious exclamation
jokes.explanation: Good Fry-specific reference combining pop culture with his tendency toward weird exclamations.

jokes.text: And I'm the Leela! Dude, over here!
jokes.setup: They're in a shrine dedicated to Leela's life
jokes.punchline: Bender makes it about himself by claiming to be 'the Leela'
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender narcissism - even in Leela's shrine he tries to make it about himself.

jokes.text: And also, for some reason, the letter I wrote you full of my personal feelings.
jokes.setup: Fry discovers his flushed belongings in the mutant collection
jokes.punchline: Admits to flushing a love letter, revealing his weird disposal methods
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Fry's social awkwardness extends to disposing of emotional expression via toilet.

jokes.text: Four more years! Four more years!
jokes.setup: Mutant leader mentions running for reelection as supreme mutant
jokes.punchline: Standard political campaign chant in absurd context
jokes.explanation: Standard political satire. The mundane campaign slogan in weird context is mildly amusing.

jokes.text: Can we really never return to this dank pit of sewage?
jokes.setup: They're being exiled from the sewers
jokes.punchline: Fry phrases it as if they're missing out on something wonderful
jokes.explanation: Good ironic framing - making exile from sewers sound like a loss. Classic Fry perspective.

jokes.text: Underdog's groin, Bart Simpson's left ear and right shin... Garfield's owner John's forehead.
jokes.setup: Explaining construction of escape balloon from parade float parts
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and bizarre body parts from cartoon characters
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and absurd catalog of balloon parts. 'Underdog's groin' is particularly inspired.

jokes.text: I used to be a little blond girl named Virginia.
jokes.setup: Fry comments that mutation worked on him
jokes.punchline: Grotesque mutant reveals previous identity as innocent child
jokes.explanation: Effective dark humor - the contrast between current appearance and former identity as 'little blond girl' is stark.

jokes.text: That's impossible, because my time is worthless!
jokes.setup: Orphanarium head says information would be waste of Fry's time
jokes.punchline: Fry enthusiastically agrees his time has no value
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry self-awareness about his own uselessness. His enthusiasm about being worthless is very in-character.

jokes.text: Would you like some dirt on any other orphans? I'm trying to save someone here. I'll have to come back for those later.
jokes.setup: Orphanarium head offers to help Fry
jokes.punchline: Offers gossip about children, Fry shows interest despite being on urgent mission
jokes.explanation: Good character moments for both - inappropriate offer of child gossip and Fry's easily distracted nature.

jokes.text: Doesn't that machine make noses? It can do other things. Why shouldn't it?
jokes.setup: Fry asks if the nose machine can translate languages
jokes.punchline: Professor's illogical justification for machine's capabilities
jokes.explanation: Good Professor logic - his machines do whatever the plot needs through circular reasoning.

jokes.text: Is it done? Certainly not. Two dings means it's done. But not like that. Slightly more rapid.
jokes.setup: Machine dings once, Fry asks if it's finished
jokes.punchline: Professor's overly specific correction about ding timing
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist humor about machine operation specifics. Professor's pedantry about ding speed is amusing.

jokes.text: Good news, Leela! I landed the contract to empty all the spittoons in Little Italy.
jokes.setup: Bender excitedly reports business success to Leela
jokes.punchline: His 'great news' is cleaning disgusting spittoons
jokes.explanation: Good Bender characterization - his excitement about gross work and poor timing during serious moment.

jokes.text: In my professional opinion, as an ear, ear, ear, nose and throat doctor...
jokes.setup: Doctor gives professional medical opinion
jokes.punchline: Has three ears, so lists 'ear' three times in specialty
jokes.explanation: Clever visual pun - doctor's mutation directly affects his medical specialty description. Well-integrated character design.

jokes.text: But if my PhD. in exolinguistics is worth diddlypoop...
jokes.setup: Father explains fake alien note will convince humans
jokes.punchline: Academic using childish euphemism for his credentials
jokes.explanation: Nice contrast between academic credentials and juvenile language. Shows character trying to be dignified but failing.

jokes.text: Don't say anything stupid, Morris.
jokes.setup: Parents are hiding from adult Leela to avoid detection
jokes.punchline: Mother preemptively warns father not to blow their cover
jokes.explanation: Good relationship dynamic - suggests father has history of saying wrong thing at crucial moments.

jokes.text: A squeezably soft paper used mainly in the sewers.
jokes.setup: Professor's analysis reveals letter was on toilet paper
jokes.punchline: Fry's euphemistic description of toilet paper quality
jokes.explanation: Fry trying to deliver serious news delicately but still referencing toilet paper commercial slogans.

tmdb_overview: Leela finally learns the truth about her parents and her own identity.
